name: Bubblegum.Graph
comment:
Graph helps to represent a directed acyclic graph consisting of a set of nodes and a set of edges.

This is an **experimental** implementation which attempts to expose a graph as a list of paths, similar in concept to xpath.
In other words, the graph is converted to a flat list to make processing easier.

This library has been implemented with the idea of of been used for the Bubblegum UI library. However, it should be possible to use it for other purposes.

If you are looking for a general purpose Graph library, I would recommend [the elm-community/graph one](https://github.com/elm-community/graph) instead.

Limits of Bubblegum.Graph:
 * No more than 1000 nodes or edges.
 * Only one edge with the same source and destination.
 * The graph should not have any cycles (loops).

# Build
@docs create, Graph

# Query
@docs findNode, findEdge, findEdgesBySource, findEdgesByDestination, findRelations, toPaths

aliases.name: Graph
aliases.comment:
The representation of a graph.

 * nodes and edges hold the main data model and are implemented as dictionaries to facilitate quick direct access.
 * relations is special view of the graph to explore parent/child relationships (inbound/outbound).
 * paths is a special view of the graph to expose the graph as a list of paths.
aliases.type: { nodes : Dict String (Node nData), edges : Dict ( String, String ) (Edge eData), relations : Dict String Relations, paths : GraphPaths }

values.name: create
values.comment: Create graph providing a list of nodes and edges.
values.type: List (Node nData) -> List (Edge eData) -> Graph nData eData

values.name: findEdge
values.comment:
Find the edge by source and destination. There should be no more than one edge for same source and destination.

    findEdge graph ("Zeus", "Athena")
values.type: Graph nData eData -> ( String, String ) -> Maybe (Edge eData)

values.name: findEdgesByDestination
values.comment:
Find edge models by destination.

    findEdgesByDestination graph "Athena"
values.type: Graph nData eData -> String -> List (Edge eData)

values.name: findEdgesBySource
values.comment:
Find edge models by source.

    findEdgesBySource graph "Zeus"
values.type: Graph nData eData -> String -> List (Edge eData)

values.name: findNode
values.comment:
Find a node from the graph.

    findNode graph "Zeus"
values.type: Graph nData eData -> String -> Maybe (Node nData)

values.name: findRelations
values.comment:
Find the relations given the nodeId.

    findRelations graph "Athena"
values.type: Graph nData eData -> String -> Maybe Relations

values.name: toPaths
values.comment: Export the graph as a list of paths faciliting a xpath like approach.

name: Bubblegum.GraphPaths

aliases.name: GraphPaths
aliases.comment:
Representation of graph as a list of paths.

  * paths represent the list of paths.
  * idToPath provides a quick conversion between pathId and its value.
aliases.type: { paths : List Path, idToPath : Dict String Path }

values.name: byId
values.comment:
Retrieve a path by id 

    byid graphPaths "001/011/111"

values.name: byNodeIds
values.comment:
Retrieve a path by providing a list of nodeIds 

    byNodeIds graphPaths ["Athena", "Zeus"]

values.name: children
values.comment: Selects all children

values.name: create
values.comment: Creates an object representing an enumeration of paths in the graph

values.name: descendants
values.comment: Selects all descendants

values.name: descendantsOrSelf
values.comment: Selects all descendants and itself

values.name: parent
values.comment: Selects the parent if any

name: Bubblegum.ValueKey
comment:
ValueKey helps to represent the key of value in a ValueAccess object.

# Format

A value key has a format like "004/006/007/009:006/007/008/009". 
The values on left of the colon represents the path (list of nodeIds), sorted from ancestors to children.
The values on right of the colon represents the indices.
Internally, the ValueKey object store these values in reverse order, to make it easier to find the parent by applying List.tail.


# Assumptions
 
Indices and nodeIds should not exceed 1000 (3 digits). In practice, this should not be an issue.
This means the graph should not exceed 1000 nodes, otherwise some of the functions may return unexpected values.
The benefit of this approach is that the length of the key is therefore determined by the number of nodes in the path.

# Conversion
@docs fromStr, toStr

# Operations on indices
@docs childIndice, incrementKey

# Predicates
@docs isDescendantOrSelf, isDescendant, isAncestor

values.name: childIndice
values.comment:
Return the indice of the child node.
  
    childIndice  "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009" == 9

values.name: fromStr
values.comment:
Convert a string to value key.
  
    fromStr "004/006/007/009:006/007/008/009" == {path = [9,7,6,4], indices = [9,8,7,6]}

values.name: isDescendant
values.comment:
Check if a key is a descendant of another key.
  
    isDescendant "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009" "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009"

values.name: isDescendantOrSelf
values.comment:
Check if a key is a descendant or self of another key.
  
    isDescendantOrSelf "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009" "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009"

values.name: toStr
values.comment:
Convert a string to value key.
  
    toStr {path = [9,7,6,4], indices = [9,8,7,6]} ==  "004/006/007/009:006/007/008/009"

name: Bubblegum.Node

aliases.name: Node
aliases.comment:
The core representation of a node or vertex.

    {id = "London", value = "8,787,892 inhabitants"}

values.name: create
values.comment:
Creates a node or vertex providing the id and value.

    create "London" "8,787,892 inhabitants"

values.name: fromTuple
values.comment:
Convert a tuple to a node.

    fromTuple ("London", "8,787,892 inhabitants") == {id = "London", value = "8,787,892 inhabitants"}

values.name: toTuple
values.comment:
Convert a node to a tuple.

    toTuple {id = "London", value = "8,787,892 inhabitants"} == ("London", "8,787,892 inhabitants")

name: Bubblegum.Path

aliases.name: Path
aliases.comment:
Representation of path in the Graph.

    { id = "002/001", nodeIds = ["Athena", "Zeus"] }

 * id is a unique id representing this path in the graph. The expected format is a list of integers seperated by slashes. Each integer represents a nodeIds sorted from parents to children.
 * nodeIds represent a list of nodeIds, sorted from children to parent.

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create a path providing an unique id and a list of node ids, children first. 

    create (Dict.fromList [("Athena", 1), ("Zeus", 2)]  ["Athena", "Zeus"]
values.type: Dict String Int -> List String -> Path

name: Bubblegum.ValueDict

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert an association list into values .

values.name: get
values.comment:
Get the value associated with a value-key. If the key is not found, return
`Nothing`.

    get values "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009" == ("004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009", "some value")
values.type: Values m -> String -> Maybe ( String, m )

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a value into values. Replaces value when there is
a collision. This will take *O(log n)* time.

    insert values ("004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009", "some value")
values.type: Values m -> ( String, m ) -> Values m

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
Determine if the dictionary is empty.

    isEmpty empty == True

values.name: keys
values.comment:
Get all of the keys in values, sorted from lowest to highest.

    keys values

values.name: member
values.comment:
Check whether there is value-key in values.

    member values "004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009" == True

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Remove a key-value pair from values. If the key is not found,
no changes are made. This will take *O(log n)* time.

    remove values ("004/006/007/011:006/007/008/009", "some value")

values.name: size
values.comment: Determine the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert values into an association list of key-value pairs, sorted by keys.

values.name: union
values.comment:
Combine two values objects. If there is a collision, preference is given
to the first values object.
values.type: Values m -> Values m -> Values m

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the value of a values object for a specific key with a given function.
values.type: Values m -> String -> (Maybe ( String, m ) -> Maybe ( String, m )) -> Values m

name: Bubblegum.Edge

aliases.name: Edge
aliases.type: { source : String, destination : String, value : eData }

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create an edge providing the source, the destination and the value.

    ldnParis = create "London" "Paris" "344 km"

values.name: fromTuple
values.comment:
Convert a tuple to an edge.

    fromTuple (("London", "Paris"), "344 km") == { source = "London", destination = "Paris", value = "344 km"}
values.type: ( ( String, String ), Edge eData ) -> Edge eData

values.name: toTuple
values.comment:
Convert an edge to a tuple.

    toTuple ldnParis == (("London", "Paris"), "344 km")
values.type: Edge eData -> ( ( String, String ), Edge eData )

name: Bubblegum.Irrelevant

unions.name: Irrelevant
unions.comment: Data which is not relevant or applicable in this given situation

name: Bubblegum.Relations

aliases.name: Relations
aliases.comment:
The representation of the inbound and outbound relationships to a node.
  
    { inbound = [("London", "Paris"), ("Amsterdam", "Paris")], outbound = [("Paris", "New York")] }
aliases.type: { inbound : List ( String, String ), outbound : List ( String, String ) }

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create the relations against a node providing the inbound and outbound values.

    create [("London", "Paris"), ("Amsterdam", "Paris")] [("Paris", "New York")]
values.type: List ( String, String ) -> List ( String, String ) -> Relations

values.name: isLeaf
values.comment:
Test whether the relations represents a leaf in the graph. No more children.

    isLeaf { inbound = [("London", "Paris"), ("Amsterdam", "Paris")], outbound = [] } == True

values.name: isRoot
values.comment:
Test whether the relations represents a root in the graph. No more parents.

    isRoot { inbound = [], outbound = [("Paris", "New York")] } == True
